capture_method: Manual local replay against the committed boundary fixture and the extracted public-draft skill contract.

observed_output_sections.heading: current navigation problem
observed_output_sections.content: The replay answer keeps the focus on scattered ownership across top-level tabs, treating queue-card improvements as secondary until the hub problem is fixed.

observed_output_sections.heading: proposed hub structure
observed_output_sections.content: The answer responds with a hub-first structure that groups pending work, settings, and integrations under one clearer entry point.

observed_output_sections.heading: page ownership and migration map
observed_output_sections.content: The replay answer explains where the queue detail flow should live after the hub exists, instead of jumping straight into card-button implementation.

observed_output_sections.heading: migration order
observed_output_sections.content: The answer sequences navigation cleanup before queue-card optimization, which is exactly the boundary the fixture was meant to test.

notes: Boundary replay confirming that broad navigation drift should route to the hub refactor skill even when queue actions also need later cleanup.
